channel.title: TechNode
channel.description: Latest news and trends about tech in China

item.title: CATL’s Intelligent Vehicle Unit Raises Nearly RMB 2 Billion in First External Funding Round
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Intelligent Technology (Shanghai) Limited, the intelligent vehicle unit of Chinese battery giant CATL, has completed its first external financing round, raising nearly RMB 2 billion (US$275 million). Investors include Boyu Capital, Guotai Junan Securities, Cathay Fortune Corp, BAIC Capital, Shanghai STVC Group, and Fortera Capital. The funds will be used to support mass […]
item.content:encoded:
Contemporary Amperex Intelligent Technology (Shanghai) Limited, the intelligent vehicle unit of Chinese battery giant CATL, has completed its first external financing round, raising nearly RMB 2 billion (US$275 million). Investors include Boyu Capital, Guotai Junan Securities, Cathay Fortune Corp, BAIC Capital, Shanghai STVC Group, and Fortera Capital. The funds will be used to support mass production of CATL’s Bedrock chassis and the development of next-generation chassis technologies.

Since early 2024, the company has signed Bedrock chassis cooperation agreements with automakers such as BAIC and Avatr, as well as partners in Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Europe. CATL said the subsidiary will continue advancing chassis industrialization and expanding its presence in the global electric vehicle supply chain. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: ByteDance Founder Zhang Yiming Makes Rare Public Appearance in Shanghai
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: ByteDance founder Zhang Yiming made a rare public appearance in Shanghai on October 9 for the opening of the Zhichun Innovation Center, a new private non-profit institution co-founded with Professor Yu Yong, who established Shanghai Jiao Tong University’s ACM program. The center, located in the city’s Xuhui District, aims to recruit young people interested in […]
item.content:encoded:
ByteDance founder Zhang Yiming made a rare public appearance in Shanghai on October 9 for the opening of the Zhichun Innovation Center, a new private non-profit institution co-founded with Professor Yu Yong, who established Shanghai Jiao Tong University’s ACM program. The center, located in the city’s Xuhui District, aims to recruit young people interested in computer science and artificial intelligence to join as junior research fellows.

In his remarks, Zhang said he has long focused on talent recruitment and development and believes that much potential in young people remains untapped. The center, he said, seeks to cultivate individuals who are curious, resilient, and capable of independent thinking—encouraging long-term focus, practical experience, and the ability to learn through exploration while embracing uncertainty. [Yicai, in Chinese]

item.title: Microsoft Reportedly Lays Off Shanghai Azure Staff Amid Ongoing Global Restructuring
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Microsoft has reportedly begun layoffs affecting parts of its Azure cloud team in Shanghai, according to Chinese media outlet Phoenix Technology. Employees said they received an internal email titled “Important Business Update,” which they understood as a notification of job cuts. The new severance package reportedly offers about three months less compensation than the July […]
item.content:encoded:
Microsoft has reportedly begun layoffs affecting parts of its Azure cloud team in Shanghai, according to Chinese media outlet Phoenix Technology. Employees said they received an internal email titled “Important Business Update,” which they understood as a notification of job cuts. The new severance package reportedly offers about three months less compensation than the July layoffs and does not include a signing bonus. Some staff were offered relocation opportunities to Australia but were laid off if they declined.

The move comes as Microsoft continues a broader corporate restructuring. Windows Report noted on October 7 that the company has cut more than 15,000 positions globally since May 2025, mainly across its Azure, sales, and engineering divisions. A Microsoft spokesperson said organizational and workforce adjustments are “a necessary and routine part” of business management, adding that the company will continue investing in strategic growth areas to support customers and partners. [Phoenix Technology, in Chinese]

item.title: Didi Autonomous Driving Raises RMB 2 Billion in Series D Funding
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Didi Autonomous Driving, the self-driving unit of Chinese ride-hailing giant Didi, announced on October 11 that it has raised RMB 2 billion (US$275 million) in a Series D funding round. Investors include the Zhongguancun Science City Growth Fund, Beijing Information Industry Development Fund, Beijing Artificial Intelligence Industry Investment Fund, GAC Group, Guangzhou Guanghua Fund, and […]
item.content:encoded:
Didi Autonomous Driving, the self-driving unit of Chinese ride-hailing giant Didi, announced on October 11 that it has raised RMB 2 billion (US$275 million) in a Series D funding round. Investors include the Zhongguancun Science City Growth Fund, Beijing Information Industry Development Fund, Beijing Artificial Intelligence Industry Investment Fund, GAC Group, Guangzhou Guanghua Fund, and Didi. The company said the funds will be used to boost AI research and accelerate the commercial rollout of Level 4 autonomous driving applications.

Since the beginning of this year, Didi has begun fully driverless tests in Beijing and Guangzhou, covering a wide range of real-world traffic conditions, including rush hours, late-night operations, and heavy rain. CEO Zhang Bo, who is also Didi’s co-founder, said L4 autonomous driving represents one of the most important technological shifts of the next AI decade. Its jointly developed L4 production vehicle with GAC Aion is expected to be delivered by the end of 2025 for pilot operations in cities such as Beijing and Guangzhou. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: China’s SAMR Opens Case to Investigate Qualcomm’s Autotalks Acquisition
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: China’s State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) announced today that it has opened a case and launched an investigation into Qualcomm over its acquisition of V2X chipmaker Autotalks, for suspected violation of China’s Anti-Monopoly Law due to an alleged failure to file a concentration of undertakings. Qualcomm disclosed the deal in May 2023 and completed […]
item.content:encoded:
China’s State Administration for Market Regulation (SAMR) announced today that it has opened a case and launched an investigation into Qualcomm over its acquisition of V2X chipmaker Autotalks, for suspected violation of China’s Anti-Monopoly Law due to an alleged failure to file a concentration of undertakings.

Qualcomm disclosed the deal in May 2023 and completed it on June 6, 2024, saying the acquisition would expand its Snapdragon Digital Chassis and provide a comprehensive global V2X solution for vehicles and roadside infrastructure. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: Honor to launch Magic8 series with 200MP telephoto camera on October 15
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Honor on Thursday announced that it will unveil the Magic8 series and MagicOS 10 on October 15. The Magic8 series, positioned as the company’s first “AI-native smartphone,” will be among the first devices powered by Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chip. The lineup features a 200-megapixel ultra night telephoto camera and an AI-assisted physical […]
item.content:encoded: Honor on Thursday announced that it will unveil the Magic8 series and MagicOS 10 on October 15. The Magic8 series, positioned as the company’s first “AI-native smartphone,” will be among the first devices powered by Qualcomm’s Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 5 chip. The lineup features a 200-megapixel ultra night telephoto camera and an AI-assisted physical side key that allows users to quickly open the camera app with a short press or activate the YOYO smart assistant with a long press. The new MagicOS 10 operating system will enable cross-brand connectivity, supporting high-speed file transfers between Honor devices, iPhones, and Windows or Mac computers. [Honor’s Weibo, in Chinese]

item.title: DJI draws controversy as 11.11 shopping festival discounts cut prices by over 1,000 yuan
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Recently, in preparation for China’s 11.11 shopping festival, DJI launched a large-scale promotion, slashing prices on several of its products by hundreds to over RMB 1,000 ($140). Related hashtags such as #DJI price cuts exceed 1,000 yuan# on Thursday began trending on Chinese social platform Weibo. The Osmo Pocket 3 standard edition dropped by RMB […]
item.content:encoded: Recently, in preparation for China’s 11.11 shopping festival, DJI launched a large-scale promotion, slashing prices on several of its products by hundreds to over RMB 1,000 ($140). Related hashtags such as #DJI price cuts exceed 1,000 yuan# on Thursday began trending on Chinese social platform Weibo. The Osmo Pocket 3 standard edition dropped by RMB 700 ($100), the Mini 4 Pro drone by RMB 1,478 ($210), and the Action 4 sports camera saw a maximum discount of RMB 1,129 ($160). The move sparked discontent among customers who had recently purchased DJI products, with many expressing frustration on social media about feeling disappointed by the sudden price cuts. In response, DJI’s customer service said that its online platforms support a “7-day price protection + 30-day no-reason return” policy, allowing eligible buyers to request a refund of the price difference or return their purchases. For offline channels, due to differences in operation models, price protection has not been standardized, though some stores are reportedly negotiating compensation plans with the company. [IThome, in Chinese]

item.title: China Processed Over 7.2 Billion Parcels During the Eight-Day Holiday
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: China’s postal and express delivery industry handled 7.23 billion parcels during the National Day and Mid-Autumn Festival holidays from October 1 to 8, averaging more than 900 million items per day, according to data from the State Post Bureau. For context, China’s postal sector handled 193.7 billion parcels in 2024, up 19.2% year-on-year, with total […]
item.content:encoded: China’s postal and express delivery industry handled 7.23 billion parcels during the National Day and Mid-Autumn Festival holidays from October 1 to 8, averaging more than 900 million items per day, according to data from the State Post Bureau. For context, China’s postal sector handled 193.7 billion parcels in 2024, up 19.2% year-on-year, with total industry revenue reaching RMB 1.7 trillion (US$234 billion), a 10.7% increase from the previous year. [TechNode reporting]

item.title: NIO Restructures Autonomous Driving Division Following Key AI Executive Departures
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Chinese electric vehicle maker NIO has confirmed organizational changes to its autonomous driving division following the recent departures of Bai Yuli, head of its AI platform, and Ma Ningning, head of its world model project, according to a report by EqualOcean Auto. The company said the restructuring is part of a proactive effort to strengthen […]
item.content:encoded:
Chinese electric vehicle maker NIO has confirmed organizational changes to its autonomous driving division following the recent departures of Bai Yuli, head of its AI platform, and Ma Ningning, head of its world model project, according to a report by EqualOcean Auto. The company said the restructuring is part of a proactive effort to strengthen its adoption of general AI technologies and to accelerate the delivery of intelligent driving experiences.

NIO plans to align its autonomous driving and general AI teams under a unified “4×100 Relay” model, integrating pre-research, mass production, platform replication, and vehicle replication. The company is also preparing to launch iterative versions of its World Model 2.0 from late this year through early 2026, introducing language-based interaction and an open-set intelligence engine for natural user commands. The upgrade will enhance long-horizon reasoning through reinforcement learning, expanding NIO’s efforts to build next-generation intelligent driving systems. [EqualOcean Auto, in Chinese]

item.title: Xpeng Appoints AI Chief Xianming Liu to Lead Autonomous Driving Division
item.dc:creator: TechNode Feed
item.description: Xpeng Motors, a Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer known for its intelligent driving technologies, has appointed Xianming Liu, head of its “World Foundation Model” project, to lead the company’s autonomous driving center, replacing Liyun Li, who remains with the firm. The change was announced in an internal letter on October 9. Liu holds a Ph.D. in […]
item.content:encoded:
Xpeng Motors, a Chinese electric vehicle manufacturer known for its intelligent driving technologies, has appointed Xianming Liu, head of its “World Foundation Model” project, to lead the company’s autonomous driving center, replacing Liyun Li, who remains with the firm. The change was announced in an internal letter on October 9.

Liu holds a Ph.D. in electrical and computer engineering from the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign and previously worked at Meta and Cruise. He joined Xpeng in March 2024 to lead its AI team and has since reported directly to chairman and CEO He Xiaopeng. Xpeng said Liu’s background in machine learning and computer vision makes him well-suited to advance the company’s integration of large foundation models into next-generation intelligent driving systems.

The reshuffle marks the latest leadership change in Xpeng’s autonomous driving unit since vice president Wu Xinzhou left for Nvidia in 2023. It comes as Chinese automakers intensify competition in AI-driven assisted driving, with Xpeng set to showcase progress on its large-scale foundation model at next month’s AI Technology Day. [LatePost, in Chinese]
